description: Declarative MCP server registry for aiox-ads squad. This file defines WHAT MCPs exist and their capabilities -- it does NOT contain secrets or tokens.

servers.name: meta-ads-mcp
servers.version_note: Pinned -- Pipeboard had 5 releases in 6 days. NEVER use @latest.
servers.description: Primary MCP for all Meta (Facebook/Instagram) read and write operations. Handles campaign CRUD, ad set management, audience targeting, creative uploads, pixel events, and reporting via Meta Marketing API.

env_vars.name: META_ADS_ACCESS_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Meta Business long-lived access token (Pipeboard-specific). Obtain at: https://pipeboard.co/settings/tokens

rate_limits.note: Shares quota with Meta Business account. Separate token from ads-mcp to avoid double-counting against rate limit.

health_check.expected: Returns array of ad account objects

servers.name: adspirer
servers.version_note: Pinned -- early-stage package, API surface may change between minors.
servers.description: Cross-platform ads MCP covering Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, TikTok Ads, and Meta as fallback. Use for any non-Meta platform, or as Meta fallback when Pipeboard is unavailable.

env_vars.name: GOOGLE_ADS_DEVELOPER_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Google Ads API developer token. Obtain at: https://ads.google.com/aw/apicenter

env_vars.name: GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_ID
env_vars.description: Google OAuth2 client ID. Obtain at: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials

env_vars.name: GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_SECRET
env_vars.description: Google OAuth2 client secret. Same credentials page as CLIENT_ID.

env_vars.name: GOOGLE_ADS_REFRESH_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Google OAuth2 refresh token. Generated during OAuth flow.

env_vars.name: GOOGLE_ADS_CUSTOMER_ID
env_vars.description: Google Ads customer/account ID (format: 123-456-7890). Find in Google Ads top-right corner.

env_vars.name: META_ADS_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECONDARY
env_vars.description: Separate Meta access token for ads-mcp fallback operations. MUST be a different token than Pipeboard to avoid double rate-limit consumption. Obtain at: https://developers.facebook.com/tools/explorer/

env_vars.name: LINKEDIN_ADS_ACCESS_TOKEN
env_vars.description: LinkedIn Marketing API OAuth2 access token. Obtain at: https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps

env_vars.name: TIKTOK_ADS_ACCESS_TOKEN
env_vars.description: TikTok Marketing API access token. Obtain at: https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/developer

rate_limits.note: Each platform has its own rate limit. Meta fallback token MUST be separate from Pipeboard token.

health_check.expected: Returns campaign list or empty array for any configured platform

meta.note: Always route Meta operations to Pipeboard (meta-ads-mcp) first. Use adspirer only if Pipeboard is down or rate-limited.

google.note: Google Ads exclusively via adspirer. No alternative MCP.

conflict_rules.description: Never two MCPs writing to the same campaign/ad simultaneously. If meta-ads-mcp is performing a write operation on a Meta campaign, adspirer MUST NOT write to the same campaign until the first operation completes.
conflict_rules.enforcement: campaign-manager checks active_mcp_lock before dispatching any write operation

conflict_rules.description: Pipeboard and adspirer MUST use separate Meta access tokens to avoid exhausting the rate limit twice as fast. One token per MCP.
conflict_rules.enforcement: setup-guide validates token uniqueness during Phase 3

conflict_rules.description: All MCP versions MUST be pinned to exact semver. Never use @latest, @next, or version ranges. Update only via explicit story/task.
conflict_rules.enforcement: bootstrap.sh validates pinned versions on setup
